lines.line: # Introduction to Dr. Anne Patrice Burgess and Her Medical Background

> The interview begins with Hannah Laurie Hine, state historian for the Idaho State Historical Society, introducing herself and Dr. Anne Patrice Burgess, who is an accomplished physician and chair of the Idaho Covid 19 Vaccine Advisory Committee. The date and location of the interview are provided, and the purpose is explained as part of a larger Covid 19 oral history project. Dr. Burgess shares her background, including her education in Idaho, her initial interest in chemical engineering, and her eventual shift to medicine inspired by her faith and a desire to help people.

metadata.title: Introduction to Dr. Anne Patrice Burgess and Her Medical Background

The date and location of the interview are provided, and the purpose is explained as part of a larger Covid 19 oral history project. Dr. Burgess shares her background, including her education in Idaho, her initial interest in chemical engineering, and her eventual shift to medicine inspired by her faith and a desire to help people.

lines.line: # Reflecting on Idaho's Response Over Three Years

> The speaker reflects on Idaho's response to the pandemic over the years, expressing pride in the state's actions in 2020 and the difficult experiences of 2021, including the shift from healthcare heroes to public anger. She discusses the ongoing challenges in 2022, such as staffing shortages and financial recovery for healthcare systems.

metadata.title: Reflecting on Idaho's Response Over Three Years
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on Idaho's response to the pandemic over the years, expressing pride in the state's actions in 2020 and the difficult experiences of 2021, including the shift from healthcare heroes to public anger. She discusses the ongoing challenges in 2022, such as staffing shortages and financial recovery for healthcare systems.

lines.line: # Initial Reactions to COVID-19 and Stay-at-Home Order

> The speaker discusses her initial awareness of COVID-19 through medical circles and the impact of the pandemic overseas. She recalls the surreal experience of Idaho's stay-at-home order and the confusion it caused in her medical practice. The speaker also reflects on the challenges of adapting to new safety protocols and the uncertainty surrounding the duration of the pandemic.

metadata.title: Initial Reactions to COVID-19 and Stay-at-Home Order
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses her initial awareness of COVID-19 through medical circles and the impact of the pandemic overseas. She recalls the surreal experience of Idaho's stay-at-home order and the confusion it caused in her medical practice. The speaker also reflects on the challenges of adapting to new safety protocols and the uncertainty surrounding the duration of the pandemic.

lines.line: # Adapting Clinical Practice During the Pandemic

> In her clinical practice, the speaker faced challenges such as determining the necessity of in-person visits and implementing telemedicine. She describes the transition to virtual appointments, the support from technical staff, and the sense of connection and reassurance it provided to patients during the lockdown.

metadata.title: Adapting Clinical Practice During the Pandemic
metadata.synopsis: In her clinical practice, the speaker faced challenges such as determining the necessity of in-person visits and implementing telemedicine. She describes the transition to virtual appointments, the support from technical staff, and the sense of connection and reassurance it provided to patients during the lockdown.

lines.line: # Leading the COVID-19 Vaccine Advisory Committee

> Dr. Burgess discusses her role as chair of the COVID-19 Vaccine Advisory Committee, including the process of assuming the role and the time commitment involved. She reflects on the committee's achievements in vaccine prioritization and the challenges they faced due to limited vaccine supply and public misinformation.

metadata.title: Leading the COVID-19 Vaccine Advisory Committee
metadata.synopsis: Dr. Burgess discusses her role as chair of the COVID-19 Vaccine Advisory Committee, including the process of assuming the role and the time commitment involved. She reflects on the committee's achievements in vaccine prioritization and the challenges they faced due to limited vaccine supply and public misinformation.

lines.line: # Saint Alphonsus's Organizational Response to COVID-19

> Dr. Burgess outlines her leadership roles at Saint Alphonsus during the pandemic, including her involvement in the incident command structure and committees focused on lab testing and medical staff support. She highlights the benefits of being part of a larger health system, such as resource sharing and collaboration, which were crucial in addressing the challenges posed by the pandemic.

metadata.title: Saint Alphonsus's Organizational Response to COVID-19
metadata.synopsis: Dr. Burgess outlines her leadership roles at Saint Alphonsus during the pandemic, including her involvement in the incident command structure and committees focused on lab testing and medical staff support. She highlights the benefits of being part of a larger health system, such as resource sharing and collaboration, which were crucial in addressing the challenges posed by the pandemic.

lines.line: # Public Perception and Media's Role During the Vaccine Rollout

> The speaker addresses the public's perception of the vaccines and the misinformation that contributed to vaccine hesitancy. She emphasizes the committee's efforts to maintain transparency and the proactive role of the media in disseminating accurate information. The speaker also touches on the historical context of vaccine hesitancy and the challenges posed by social media amplification.

metadata.title: Public Perception and Media's Role During the Vaccine Rollout
metadata.synopsis: The speaker addresses the public's perception of the vaccines and the misinformation that contributed to vaccine hesitancy. She emphasizes the committee's efforts to maintain transparency and the proactive role of the media in disseminating accurate information. The speaker also touches on the historical context of vaccine hesitancy and the challenges posed by social media amplification.

lines.line: > The speaker vividly recalls the early vaccine clinic experiences, describing the festive atmosphere with Christmas movies playing and the sense of hope that the vaccine brought to healthcare workers. She details the logistical challenges of vaccine distribution, the expansion of the clinic, and the involvement of volunteers, including the National Guard and FEMA staff.

metadata.title: Experiences from the Vaccine Clinic
metadata.synopsis: The speaker vividly recalls the early vaccine clinic experiences, describing the festive atmosphere with Christmas movies playing and the sense of hope that the vaccine brought to healthcare workers. She details the logistical challenges of vaccine distribution, the expansion of the clinic, and the involvement of volunteers, including the National Guard and FEMA staff.

lines.line: # Concluding Thoughts on the Pandemic's Impact

> In the concluding part of the interview, the speaker shares her pride in the healthcare workers and the collaborative efforts during the pandemic. She expresses hope for a more respectful relationship between the public and healthcare professionals and a better understanding of science in the future.

# Reflecting on Idaho's Response Over Three Years

> The speaker reflects on Idaho's response to the pandemic over the years, expressing pride in the state's actions in 2020 and the difficult experiences of 2021, including the shift from healthcare heroes to public anger. story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Ann Patrice Burgess
story.description: The interview provides an extensive look into Idaho's response to the COVID-19 pandemic, focusing on Dr. Ann Patrice Burgess’s experiences. Topics covered include her background and role as chair of the Idaho COVID-19 Vaccine Advisory Committee, the logistical and emotional challenges faced during the pandemic, and the intricacies of the vaccine rollout. Discussions touch on supply limitations, vaccine hesitancy, and misconceptions, alongside the personal and professional toll on healthcare workers. The interview highlights the collaboration and resilience within the healthcare system while acknowledging the profound impact of misinformation and societal polarization on public health efforts.
